sections.name: Introduction
topics: TurnoutBoss board overview and feature list
Signaling around a simple passing siding
Clear/Approach/Stop signal indication concepts
Double-headed signals at facing points (Normal vs Diverging routes)
sections.notes: High-level orientation. Useful background for understanding why fields exist, but does not describe specific configuration fields.

sections.name: Planning Your Installation
topics: Left vs Right board assignment (turnout end of siding)
Turnout control approaches: one-button toggle vs two-button N/D
Occupancy block setup and current-sensing detector wiring
Signal LED wiring types: common cathode, common anode, bidirectional
Turnout feedback options: zero, one, or two switches
Power supply options: LCC cable, wall-wart, terminal block; servo power 5-8V
sections.notes: Decision-oriented planning content. Strong source for relevance rules (e.g., 'if no feedback sensors, leave field default') and for recipes describing installation planning steps.

sections.name: Basic Configuration of the TurnoutBoss Boards
topics: Network preparation: connect boards, terminators, power up
Factory reset procedure (Hold Teach+Learn, press Reset, press Teach)
Configure board connections via button presses (left/right neighbor, end-of-siding role)
Configure initial hardware options via button presses (signal wiring type, one/two-button turnout control)
Button-press flow diagrams (Figures 6, 7, 8)
sections.notes: Pure step-by-step recipe content using on-board buttons. Not typically relevant to CDI field descriptions, but mirrors fields in 'How this TurnoutBoss is used on your layout' and 'Turnout Control' / 'Signalhead Lamp Configuration' groups.

sections.name: Installation and Wiring
topics: LCC cable, terminators, and external power wiring
Turnout motor (Tortoise/stall-motor) and pushbutton wiring
Servo output wiring and power requirements
Occupancy detector coil wiring and optical input
Signal head wiring (common anode/cathode/bidirectional) and built-in lamp-test mode (Teach+Reset)
Turnout feedback switch wiring (one or two switches)
sections.notes: Physical wiring steps. Useful for recipes that pair CDI choices with corresponding physical connections.

sections.name: Using a Configuration Tool
topics: Overview of CDI configuration tools (JMRI, Model Railroad System, LccTools)
Three CDI segments: Layout Configuration Setup, Hardware Configuration, Producers and Consumers
Screenshots of each segment (Figures 9, 10, 11)

Per-field descriptions for User Info, Board Usage, Layout Connections, Turnout Control, Turnout Feedback, Facing Point Signals, Signalhead Lamp Configuration, Track Detector Sensitivity, Signal LED Brightness, Servo Limits, Optical Input
Remote turnout control and control-panel integration via LCC events
sections.notes: Core source for CDI field/section semantics. Subsections 5.1-5.11 (pages 19-22) are tight prose maps to each CDI group; section 6.1/6.2 (pages 21-22) document event-driven external integration.

appendices.name: A. Event Definitions
appendices.purpose: Enumerates every event ID the TurnoutBoss produces (P) or consumes (C), grouped to match the CDI 'Producers and Consumers' segment. Includes screenshots of the event list (Figures 12-16) and prose descriptions for Occupancy (A.1), Turnout Command (A.2), Turnout Feedback (A.3), Turnout Buttons (A.4), Turnout Observed Position (A.5), Signal Outputs (A.6), Signal State P/C (A.7/A.8), Vital Logic Control (A.9), and Optical Sensor (A.10). Differentiates auto-managed events (Left/Right pair auto-wires occupancy) from user-facing copy/paste events. Primary source for profile-1 event roles and profile-4 event field descriptions.
